Summary: In an ex vivo myeloid differentiation model where HSPCs were exposed to supernatants from activated CAR‑T cells (spCAR), single‑cell RNA sequencing showed upregulation of CEBPA. Concurrently, the activity of regulons involved in neutrophil and monocytic maturation (ID2 and MAFB) decreased.
Reference: Palacios-Berraquero, M. L., Rodriguez-Marquez, P., Calleja-Cervantes, M. Molecular mechanisms promoting long-term cytopenia after BCMA CAR-T therapy in multiple myeloma. Blood Adv, 8(21), 5479-5492. https://doi.org/10.1182/bloodadvances.2023012522

Summary: Systematic elaboration present. C/EBPα is required for generation of Ly6G+ neutrophils (granulocytes) and Ly6Chi monocytes in cultured cKithi mouse bone marrow progenitors stimulated with SCF, IL‑3, and IL‑6 when Notch signaling is absent. C/EBPα maintains Ly6Chi monocyte identity by halting progression to Ly6Clo monocytes. In germline C/EBPα-deficient mice and in Cebpa +37 kb enhancer mutant mice, neutrophil development is defective. In Cebpa +37−/− progenitors, Ly6C hi monocytes fail to develop. In the cultured Cebpa +37−/− progenitors, cells instead predominantly become Ly6Clo CD11c+ monocytes while germline deficiency showed no change in overall monocyte numbers. In Cebpa +37−/− mice, Ly6G+ neutrophil‑like cells retained Ly6C at levels similar to wild‑type neutrophils. Because neutrophils do not express C/EBPα, Ly6C may be controlled by additional factors.
Reference: Kim, S., Chen, J., Ou, F., Liu, T. T., Jo, S., Gillanders, W. E., Murphy, T. L., & Murphy, K. M. (2024). Transcription factor C/EBPα is required for the development of Ly6C<sup>hi</sup> monocytes but not Ly6C<sup>lo</sup> monocytes. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A, 121(15), e2315659121. https://doi.org/10.1073/pnas.2315659121

Summary: CEBPA is a master regulator of myeloid differentiation that drives hematopoiesis toward the granulocytic lineage and is critical for neutrophil differentiation. In AML, intragenic CEBPA mutations create an N‑terminal frameshift that produces a dominant-negative p30 isoform. Other mutations disrupt the C‑terminal basic zipper and weaken DNA binding. Transcriptional repression by leukemic fusion proteins or promoter hypermethylation also leads to low CEBPA expression in some AML patients. These disruptions contribute to differentiation arrest in AML blast cells and Cebpa-null mice lack neutrophil and eosinophil production. Comparative gene profiling of CEBPA wild-type and mutant AML patient cohorts identified HK3 and KLF5 as novel CEBPA targets. The study found CEBPA-dependent activation of HK3 and KLF5 transcription during ATRA-mediated neutrophil differentiation of APL cells. The data indicate direct regulation of HK3 by CEBPA and suggest indirect regulation of KLF5 by CEBPA. In NB4 and HT93 APL cell lines treated with 1 μM ATRA for four days, lentiviral shRNA-mediated knockdown of CEBPA reduced HK3 and KLF5 mRNA levels relative to controls. The findings explain low HK3 and KLF5 expression in a particular AML subtype.
Reference: Federzoni, E. A., Humbert, M., Torbett, B. E., Behre, G., Fey, M. F., & Tschan, M. P. (2014). CEBPA-dependent HK3 and KLF5 expression in primary AML and during AML differentiation. Sci Rep, 4, 4261. https://doi.org/10.1038/srep04261

Summary:
Relationship structure: association.

In one relapse case with biallelic CEBPA mutation, acquisition of CSF3R T618I coincided with a myeloproliferative phenotype. This phenotype included near‑100% bone marrow cellularity, an increased myeloid‑erythroid ratio due to expansion of maturing neutrophilic granulopoiesis, and a blast count of about 10%.

(2025). Distribution of different classes of CSF3R mutations and co-mutational pattern in 360 myeloid neoplasia. Ann Hematol, 104(1), 263-274. https://doi.org/10.1007/s00277-025-06232-1

Summary: C/EBPα is required for formation of granulocyte‑monocyte progenitors from common myeloid progenitors. In murine hematopoiesis, the Cebpa gene is regulated by a hematopoietic‑specific +37 kb enhancer and by a more widely active +8 kb enhancer. HoxA9 directly binds and inhibits the +8 kb enhancer. This inhibition represses Cebpa expression and impairs myeloid differentiation in normal hematopoietic stem and progenitor cells and in poor‑risk AML. During granulocytic differentiation of 32Dcl3 myeloid cells, Cebpa expression increases while HoxA9 expression decreases fivefold.
Reference: Peng, L., Guo, H., Ma, P., Sun, Y., Dennison, L., Aplan, P. D., Hess, J. L., & Friedman, A. D. (2019). HoxA9 binds and represses the Cebpa +8 kb enhancer. PLoS One, 14(5), e0217604. https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pone.0217604
